title: Ensure that the --wal-dir argument is set as appropriate

input_row.title: Ensure that the --wal-dir argument is set as appropriate
input_row.description: Store etcd logs separately from etcd data.
input_row.rationale: etcd is a highly-available key value store used by Kubernetes deployments for persistent storage of all of its REST API objects. These objects are sensitive in nature and should not be mixed with log data. Keeping the log data separate from the etcd data also ensures that those two types of data could individually be safeguarded. Also, you could use a centralized and remote log directory for persistent logging. Additionally, this separation also helps to avoid IO competition between logging and other IO operations.
input_row.audit: Run the following command on the etcd server node: ps -ef | grep etcd Verify that --wal-dir argument exists, and it is set as appropriate. At the minimum, it should not be set to the same directory as set for --data-dir argument.
input_row.remediation: Edit the etcd environment file (for example, /etc/etcd/etcd.conf) on the etcd server node and set the ETCD_WAL_DIR parameter as appropriate: ETCD_WAL_DIR="<dir-name>" Edit the etcd startup file (for example, /etc/systemd/system/multi- user.target.wants/etcd.service) and configure the startup parameter for --wal-dir and set it to \"${ETCD_WAL_DIR}\": ExecStart=/bin/bash -c "GOMAXPROCS=$(nproc) /usr/bin/etcd -- name=\"${ETCD_NAME}\" --data-dir=\"${ETCD_DATA_DIR}\" --listen-client- urls=\"${ETCD_LISTEN_CLIENT_URLS}\" --wal-dir=\"${ETCD_WAL_DIR}\"" Based on your system, reload the daemon and restart the etcd service. For example, systemctl daemon-reload systemctl restart etcd.service  125 | P a g e Impact: None Default Value: By default, --wal-dir argument is not set. References: 1. https://kubernetes.io/docs/admin/etcd/ 2. https://coreos.com/etcd/docs/latest/op-guide/configuration.html#wal-dir 3. https://coreos.com/etcd/docs/latest/op-guide/configuration.html#data-dir
input_row.impact: None Default Value: By default, --wal-dir argument is not set.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Manual

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- The control requires verification of the etcd process startup arguments, specifically the --wal-dir and --data-dir flags.
- etcd is typically run as a systemd service or containerized process outside the Kubernetes API server’s direct management.
- The Kubernetes API and kubectl do NOT expose etcd process arguments or host-level configuration.
- The audit procedure explicitly requires running ps -ef | grep etcd on the etcd server node, which is a host-level operation not available via Kubernetes APIs.
- Therefore, this check cannot be automated via Kubernetes APIs or kubectl and requires manual/host-level inspection.

**MANUAL STEPS:**
1. SSH into each etcd server node in your Kubernetes cluster.
2. Run the following command to list the etcd process and its arguments:
   ```
   ps -ef | grep etcd
   ```
3. Identify the --wal-dir and --data-dir arguments in the etcd process command line.
4. Verify that:
   - The --wal-dir argument is present and set.
   - The value of --wal-dir is NOT the same as the value of --data-dir.
   - The --wal-dir is set to a directory appropriate for storing etcd WAL logs, separate from the data directory.
5. If the arguments are not set as required, review the etcd environment file (e.g., /etc/etcd/etcd.conf) and the systemd unit file (e.g., /etc/systemd/system/etcd.service) to ensure the correct configuration.
6. Confirm compliance by ensuring the WAL directory is separate from the data directory and both are set according to your organization’s security policy.

**SOURCE:** CIS KUBERNETES 1.6 BENCHMARK V1.0.0 ARCHIVE
